calendar.matters:
ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
Off Calendar Jan-20-2026 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances: Byron Sun appeard via zoom for and with Ashaduntae M. Brewster (petitioner).

From the outset of the hearing the Court shall allow abandonment of the personal items, that it will not require the reduction of statutory compensation, and that it will reject the creditors' claim.


The matter is taken under submission with the intent to grant as prayed.

Judge: Russell S. Roeca. Clerk: A. Datt. Deputy: Musto. Not reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
Off Calendar Dec-30-2025 Continued To Jan-20-26 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Byron Sun for petitioner Ashaduntae M. Brewster, who was also present (via Zoom).

The matter was called for hearing on First Amended Petition for Approval of First and Final Account and Report of Administrator with Will Annexed, etc.

The Court inquired regarding issues raised by the examiner. Mr. Sun stated that a declaration had been submitted addressing the U.S. Bank creditor's claim, representing that the account had been settled in full, but acknowledged that the filing had not yet been approved for review. With respect to the creditor's claim filed by Alex Mak Energy, Mr. Sun stated that neither he nor the personal representative had been served with the claim and that efforts to contact the claimant and locate supporting documentation had been unsuccessful. Mr. Sun requested leave to file a supplemental declaration addressing that claim.

The Court stated that upon resolution of the creditor issues, it was inclined to waive notice as requested and grant the petition, with personal property to be distributed to the four residuary beneficiaries in equal shares rather than by abandonment.

The Court continued the matter to January 20, 2026, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204, and directed petitioner to file a supplemental declaration addressing the Alex Mak Energy claim.

Judge: Russell S. Roeca; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
Off Calendar Dec-24-2025 Continued To Dec-30-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Off Calendar Oct-07-2025 Off Calendar

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Hearing For Filing Of A Petition For Final Distribution Or A Petition For Approval Of Report On Status Of Administration
Off Calendar Aug-26-2025 Continued To Oct-07-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Hearing For Filing Of A Petition For Final Distribution Or A Petition For Approval Of Report On Status Of Administration
Off Calendar Jul-29-2025 Continued To Aug-26-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED
Off Calendar Jun-18-2024 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel or Interested Person.

This matter was put on for hearing with no appearance required for the purpose of admitting the will. No one appeared to object. There being no objections, the Court granted the Petition for Letters of Administration with Will Annexed. The will dated October 15, 2020 is admitted to probate. Ashaduntae M. Kemp is appointed as the Administrator of the estate with will annexed with full authority. Bond is set at $20,000.00. Order for the same signed in open Court. A status hearing for filing of a petition for final distribution or a petition for approval of report on status of administration is set for July 29, 2025, at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Either petition is filed two weeks before the status hearing date. Karen Ann Simons is appointed probate referee.

Judge: Stephen M. Murphy. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Not reported.
